properties.remark: PRELIMINARY NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS TREE DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH A TORNADO ALONG RIVER ROAD NORTHWEST OF REPUBLIC...INCLUDING SNAPPED WHITE PINES LARGER THAN 12 INCHES IN DIAMETER. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
TREES FELL ON CABIN AND THERE WAS DAMAGE TO A CHIMNEY AT THE CABIN. TIME IS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
PRELIMINARY NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS REPORTS OF TREE DAMAGE AND A TORNADO DETECTION SIGNATURE ON RADAR SOUTH OF THE GREENWOOD RESERVOIR WERE DUE TO A TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
TRAMPOLINE IN BACKYARD WAS DAMAGED BEYOND REPAIR. LAWN FURNITURE WAS TOSSED INTO THE WOODS AND DAMAGED. APPLE TREE UPROOTED. SEVERAL LARGE SPRUCE TREES SNAPPED. DELAYED REPORT.
ESTIMATED QUARTER SIZED HAIL IN NEGAUNEE. DELYAED REPORT.
TWO TREES DOWN ON CHANEY LAKE ROAD...BLOCKING TRAFFIC IN BOTH DIRECTIONS.
LARGE TREE DOWN ON M-45
LARGE TREE DOWN ON M-45.
PEAK GUST AT MINNEAPOLIS SHOAL.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN IN THE HIGH TERRAIN IN NORTHERN HOUGHTON COUNTY. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
TREE DOWN ON POWER LINES NEAR HUBBELL TOWNSHIP.
TWO LARGE SPRUCE TREES SNAPPED AT THE TOPS. SMALL BRANCHES WERE ALSO SNAPPED. ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS TO 50 MPH.
TREE OF UNKNOWN SIZE DOWN ACROSS ROAD.
PEAK WIND GUST AT MINNEAPOLIS SHOAL LIGHT.
OTTAWA NATIONAL FOREST PARK RANGERS OBSERVED LARGE TREE LIMBS IN EXCESS OF 12 INCHES SNAPPED AT THE BLACK RIVER HARBOR RECREATIONAL AREA CAMPGROUND SITE. DELAYED REPORT.
METAL FRAMED RV STORAGE SHELTER FLATTENED BY THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS NEAR NISULA. DELAYED REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
TREE DOWN BLOCKING M-38 NEAR THE STURGEON RIVER. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
PICNIC TABLE WITH UMBRELLA BLOWN OVER BY STRONG WINDS. RAIN FALLING HORIZONTAL. .
MESONET STATION 1 SE PEQUAMING /E2255/.
CRAIG LAKE STATE PARK OBSERVED TREE DAMAGE ON CRAIG LAKE RD. IN BARAGA COUNTY. DELAYED REPORT.
LARGE TREE BRANCHES DOWN. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 60 MPH.
AT LEAST 1 INCH HAIL ESTIMATED. OBSERVER ARRIVED ON SITE AROUND 30 MINUTES AFTER HAIL WAS ESTIMATED TO HAVE FALLEN...BASED ON RADAR...AND FOUND NICKEL SIZE HAIL COVERING THE GROUND AT THAT TIME.
OBSERVED QUARTER SIZED HAIL NEAR NEGAUNEE THIS AFTERNOON. DELAYED REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
THREE FRUIT TREES OF APROX. 6 INCH DIAMETER WERE UPROOTED THIS AFTERNOON. ESTIMATED TIME CLOSE TO 4:00 P.M. EDT. DELAYED REPORT .
GOGEBIC COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTS LARGE TREES IN EXCESS OF 12 DIAMETER DOWNED ACROSS LAKE RD. NEAR UPPER POWERS RD. GOGEBIC COUNTY ROAD COMMISSION IS CLEARING THE ROAD WITH FRONT END LOADERS.

GOGEBIC COUNTY SHERIFFS REPORT THAT THE MONTREAL RIVER BRIDGE IS FLOODED ON LAKE RD. AT THE MICHIGAN AND WISCONSIN BORDER. THE GOGEBIC COUNTY ROAD COMMISSION HAS CLOSED LAKE RD. FROM NEAR SAXON WI TO LOWER POWERS ROAD.
GOGEBIC COUNTY SHERIFFS REPORT THAT THE GUARD RAIL ON LAKE RD. AT THE FLINK CREEK RAVINE HAS WASHED OUT TONIGHT.
GOGEBIC COUNTY SHERIFFS REPORT THAT THE GUARD RAIL ON LAKE RD. AT THE FLINT CREEK RAVINE HAS WASHED OUT TONIGHT.
GOGEBIC COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS HALF A BARN WAS DESTROYED IN BESSEMER TOWNSHIP ON ELDER STREET. DELAYED REPORT.
GOGEBIC COUNTY LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ON A GARAGE WITH STRUCTURAL DAMAGE ON SPRUCE STREET IN BESSEMER TOWNSHIP. DELAYED REPORT.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT DAMAGE IN AND IMMEDIATELY SURROUNDING WAKEFIELD WAS CAUSED BY STRAIGHT LINE THUNDERSTORM WINDS UP TO 90 MPH. A CAMPER TRAILER WAS OVERTURNED...A LARGE BUILDING HAD THE ROOF RIPPED OFF...A FEW OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED...SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED ON HOUSES AND VEHICLES...AND HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. SURVEY TEAM COULD NOT ACCESS ALL AREAS DUE TO THE NUMBER OF DOWNED TREES. TIME BASED ON RADAR AND INTERVIEWS OF INDIVIDUALS IN THE AREA.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS LOW END EF1 TORNADO ABOUT 3 MILES SOUTH OF BESSEMER. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT AROUND 90 MPH. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF S. BARBER RD AND SPRUCE RD AND ENDED 2.4 MILES TO THE ENE...JUST NORTHEAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF W. HARDING RD AND KOSKI RD. DAMAGE PATH WAS LESS THAN 200 YARDS WIDE AND 2.4 MILES LONG. OCCURRED JUST BEFORE MIDNIGHT CDT/1AM EDT.
GOGEBIC COUNTY LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS MULTIPLE TREES DOWN IN WAKEFIELD.
POWER LINES ARE DOWN IN WAKEFIELD ON HARRISON RD. LARGE TREES OF 30 TO 40 INCH DIAMETER HAVE SNAPPED. POWER IS OUT IN WAKEFIELD.
MESONET STATION WAKEFIELD /MC026/.
THREE LARGE TREES DOWN.
AT BARAGA 7 NW COOP LOCATION.
REGIONAL LAW ENFORCEMENT DISPATCH REPORTS A LARGE POLAR TREE WAS DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF M-38 AND PINE CREEK RD. DELAYED REPORT.
REGIONAL LAW ENFORCEMENT DISPATCH REPORTS A LARGE POPLAR TREE WAS DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF M-38 AND PINE CREEK RD. DELAYED REPORT.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS DOWNED TREES ON FROBERG RD. NEAR PELKIE. SIZE OF DOWNED TREES UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME. POWER OUTAGE REPORTED IN BARAGA.
REGIONAL LAW ENFORCEMENT DISPATCH REPORTS LARGE TREES DOWN AND SNAPPED POWER LINES NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF M-38 AND PELKIE RD. DELAYED REPORT.

ISHPEMING TWP. FIRE DEPT. REPORTS SEVERAL TREES DOWN ON COOPER LAKE RD. SOUTH OF THE DEER LAKE INTERSECTION. SOME OF THE HARDWOOD TREES DOWNED WERE ESTIMATED TO MEASURE 12 TO 18 INCHES IN DIAMETER. POWER COMPANY WAS ON THE SCENE. ONE CAR HAD A TREE LYING DOWN ON IT. DELAYED REPORT.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS DOWNED TREES AND DOWNED POWER LINES ON THE INTERSECTION OF COOPER LAKE ROAD AND DEER LAKE RD. NORTHWEST OF ISHPEMING.

SPRUCE AND ASPEN TREE DOWN ALONG COOPER LAKE RD. THESE TREES WERE 10 TO 12 INCHES IN DIAMETER. TREES ARE ALSO SNAPPED OFF TOWARD THE TOPS. THE TREE DAMAGE IS CONCENTRATED FROM THIS LOCATION TO THE NORTH AT THE COOPER LAKE RD. AND DEER LAKE RD. INTERSECTION.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN ALONG LAKE RD NEAR LITTLE GIRLS POINT.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN IN WAKEFIELD.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN AROUND LAKE GOGEBIC.
MESONET STATION WAKEFIELD /MC026/.
TREES DOWN.
DOZENS OF TREES DOWN IN AND AROUND THE VILLAGE OF ONTONAGON...INCLUDING SEVERAL DOWN ON HOMES. POWER OUT IN THE VILLAGE FOR NEARLY 8 HOURS.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN AND AROUND MASS CITY.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS THAT EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE AT EMILY LAKE CAMPGROUND WAS DUE TO STRAIGHT LINE WINDS ESTIMATED TO BE UP TO 90MPH. LOCAL REPORTS INDICATE THE STRONG WINDS LASTED AT LEAST FIVE MINUTES. DAMAGE INCLUDED TREES DOWN ON TWO CAMPERS. NO INJURIES REPORTED. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
OVER HALF ACRE OF TREES BLOWN DOWN. TREES ON CAMPERS AT EMILY LAKE CAMPGROUND AT TWIN LAKES STATE PARK. NO INJURIES.
WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE AROUND TOIVALA, DONKEN, AND TWIN LAKES. M26 IS BLOCKED BY FALLEN TREES.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ACROSS BARAGA COUNTY. TREE BLOCKING US 41 SOUTH OF LANSE.
DELAYED REPORT. MULTIPLE REPORTS OF TREES DOWN ON EITHER SIDE OF THE PORTAGE CANAL NEAR JACOBSVILLE. REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
BUOY STATION 7 WNW CALUMET /45023/.
TREES DOWN.
SEVERAL TREES REPORTED DOWN.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN BLOCKING BEAGLE CLUB RD.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN BLOCKING EAGLE CLUB RD.
MULTIPLE TREES AND LARGE LIMBS DOWN ACROSS THE CITY OF NEGAUNEE. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 50MPH FOR ABOUT 5 MINUTES. REPORTS FROM VARIOUS SOURCES.
TREE DOWN ON METROPOLITAN RD. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
MESONET STATION 9 SE FORD RIVER /XMAPV/.
TRAINED SPOTTER OBSERVED QUARTER-SIZE HAIL THIS EVENING.
OBSERVED QUARTER SIZE HAIL WITH SOME LARGER HAIL STONES GREATER THAN QUARTER SIZED. THE HAIL LASTED AN ESTIMATED 5 TO 10 MINUTES. INTENSE DOWNPOUR WAS OBSERVED.
A SECTION OF CANAL ROAD WEST OF HOUGHTON HAD A SMALL SECTION WASHOUT FROM HEAVY RAINFALL.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROADS 579 AND 388 AND TRAVELED NORTHEAST FOR 1.25 MILES, LIFTING AS IT CROSSED COUNTY ROAD 388. THE DAMAGE PATH WAS ESTIMATED TO 200 YARDS WIDE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH. SMALL FARM EQUIPMENT WAS ALSO TOSSED AROUND AS IT PASSED CLOSE TO A NEARBY FARM AND HOME.
NEAR INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 388 AND COUNTY ROAD 579 IN HERMANSVILLE. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN. VIDEO FROM SOCIAL MEDIA SHOWS TORNADO. OFFICIAL SURVEY WILL FOLLOW EARLY NEXT WEEK.
SMALL AND BRIEF WATERSPOUT OBSERVED FROM EAGLES NEST ROAD. THE SHOWER PRODUCING THE WATERSPOUT WAS APPROXIMATELY 10 MILES OFF SHORE BASED OFF RADAR AND OBSERVER ESTIMATION.

FIVE SMALL WATERSPOUTS WERE OBSERVED OFFSHORE OF THE MARQUETTE LAKEFRONT ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 31ST BETWEEN 1130 A.M. EDT AND 100 P.M. EDT. THE WATERSPOUTS WERE OBSERVED FROM NEAR MIDDLE ISLAND POINT SOUTHEAST TO NEAR HARVEY. IT IS UNKNOWN IF ANY WATERSPOUTS MOVED ONSHORE.

SEVERAL LARGE DIAMETER HARDWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED NEAR THE TRUNK OF THE TREES AT A SEASONAL CAMP NEAR LAKE SUPERIOR. THE MAST FOR THE ELECTRICAL SERVICE WAS BENT OFF. DELAYED REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. ESTIMATED TIME VIA RADAR.
3 LARGE TREES UPROOTED. EACH WERE ABOUT 2 FT IN DIAMETER. YARD FURNITURE WAS ALSO BLOWN ABOUT 50 FT AND 70 LB GRILL WAS BLOWN OVER. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
WATER RESCUE UNDERWAY ON THE CHOCOLAY RIVER AT GREEN GARDEN ROAD.
THE CHOCOLAY RIVER IS FLOODING MAGNUM ROAD. OVER 6 INCHES OF WATER OVER THE ROAD.
WATER OVER M-35 NEAR COUNTY ROAD MU

M-94 WEST IS WASHED OUT IN TWO SPOTS. ONE IS APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES WEST OF US-41 WHERE THE EAST BRANCH OF THE CHOCOLAY RIVER INTERSECTS THE ROAD. THE OTHER IS NEAR ENGMAN LAKE ROAD...WHICH IS APPROXIMATELY 3.5 MILES WEST OF US-41. INITIAL WASHOUT OCCURED SOMETIME OVERNIGHT...TIME BASED ON LATEST REPORT FROM THAT AREA.
